output_debit_classifier.reason: This transaction involves the acquisition of land with an existing building for $60,000,000, to be paid in 6 equal annual installments of $10,000,000 starting at the end of the year of acquisition. Since payment is deferred, the land should be recorded at the present value of the future payments using the 6% market interest rate. The present value of an ordinary annuity of $10,000,000 for 6 periods at 6% is approximately $49,173,240 (using PV factor of 4.917324). The existing building will be demolished immediately, so its value is considered part of the land cost (demolition costs are capitalized to land). The journal entry records: (1) Debit to Land (asset increase) for the present value of $49,173,240, and (2) Credit to Long-term Notes/Loan Payable (liability increase) for $49,173,240. The difference between the total payments ($60,000,000) and present value ($49,173,240) represents interest expense to be recognized over the 6-year payment period.
